title: StructuralAdvisory
description: A cross-record verifier observation that spans multiple records or names a pattern rather than attaching to a specific record, per design/verification-sketch-01.md V2 + PFS10-SA1..SA4. Used when the finding is not naturally a single-record review. Carries advisor_id, advised_at_τ_a, severity (closed enum matching Python's SEVERITY_*), comment (required prose), and scope (array of CrossDialectRef naming the records the pattern touches). Optional match_strength. CrossDialectRef re-inlined per PFS8-V + PFS10-X1.

cross_dialect_ref.description: Typed reference to a record in a specific dialect. Re-inlined per PFS8-V + PFS10-X1. Open dialect-token string per PFS9-X4.

record_id.description: Stable identifier for the referenced record in its dialect.

advisor_id.description: Stable identifier for the advisor emitting this finding. PFS10-SA2.

advised_at_τ_a.description: Author-time τ_a at which the advisory was recorded. PFS10-SA2.

severity.description: Closed enum matching Python's SEVERITY_* constants. Distinct from LoweringObservation severities (which have only 'noted' and 'advises-review'); StructuralAdvisory adds a third tier ('suggest-revise') for recommended revisions. PFS10-SA3.

comment.description: Required prose explaining the advisory. Empty string admitted (placeholder authoring), matching Annotation.text posture. PFS10-SA2.

scope.description: Records the advisory touches, as an array of CrossDialectRef. Empty array legal (an advisor may flag a corpus-level concern without specific record targets — banked in PFS10 OQ5). PFS10-SA4.

match_strength.description: Optional confidence score in [0, 1]. Same semantics as VerificationReview.match_strength per V7. Python default None → omitted when None.
